title: Disease burden from non-communicable diseases vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: Disease burden to non-communicable diseases (NCDs), measured in DALYs (Disability-Adjusted Life Years) per 100,000 individuals versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Disease burden from non-communicable diseases vs. GDP per capita, 2023

Disease burden to non-communicable diseases (NCDs), measured in DALYs (Disability-Adjusted Life Years) per 100,000 individuals versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

The scatter plot displays the relationship between GDP per capita, plotted on a logarithmic horizontal axis ranging from $2,000 to $100,000, and the disease burden from non-communicable diseases on the vertical axis, which spans from 0 to 50,000 DALYs per 100,000 individuals. The data points are color-coded by region, with Africa represented in purple, Asia in teal, Europe in blue, North America in orange, South America in maroon, and Oceania in light blue. A general downward trend is visible, where countries with lower GDP per capita often exhibit higher DALY rates. Nauru is positioned at the highest point on the vertical axis, exceeding 50,000 DALYs, while the Solomon Islands and Marshall Islands cluster near the 40,000 mark. In the middle range, countries such as Myanmar, Fiji, and Egypt sit between 30,000 and 35,000 DALYs. A dense grouping of African nations, including Burundi, DR Congo, and Niger, occupies the lower-left quadrant with GDP values below $2,000 and DALY rates between 20,000 and 30,000. Conversely, high-income nations like Singapore, the United States, and Germany are located on the far right with GDP figures above $50,000 and DALY rates generally falling between 10,000 and 25,000. Circle sizes vary to indicate population, with large circles visible for India, China, and the United States. The GDP per capita figures are expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices.
